text: 0.4g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a fruit, the cleanest wins are low sugar and decent fibre.
15g of carbs. the sugar load matters more than you would want. for fruit, the real question is whether the sweetness stays under control.
0.8g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. for a fruit, the best ones stay sweet without going off the rails.
vitamin c at 40% daily value, vitamin a at 12% daily value stand out most. that is only mild support overall.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

on the plus side: brings a clean fruit identity. it still contributes something valuable even when it is not elite. easy to use as a whole food. simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating. hydrating whole-fruit package. water and whole-food structure make it easier to use well than juice or sweets.

now the downsides: satiety is not especially strong. fruit often needs help from the rest of the meal. sugar can climb fast. a lot of fruits look healthy but still lean sweet. protein contribution is tiny. that is normal for fruit, but it still limits nutritional breadth.
B tier. Best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
